summary:
The presence of the second mesio-buccal canal cannot always be visually confirmed without instruments; however, studies suggest its detection by sight occurs approximately between 62%-70% of the time due to anatomical variance. This indicates significant individual differences requiring careful examination for precise diagnosis. \boxed{A}

The summary captures both core aspects—visual confirmation difficulty and approximate frequency—and aligns closely with Agent1’s conclusion while maintaining brevity as required.
